ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Receipts Of Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-29-2019 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-18-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trang La for Kelvin Siverand (Trustee).

Ms. La stated that Mr. Siverand is willing to go with $145.00 per hour in order to get this over with. The First and Final Account of Successor Trustee is under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES

Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Continued To May-15-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Feb-25-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Jan-07-2019 Off Calendar

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Jan-07-2019 Continued To Feb-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trang La for Trustee.

Ms. La stated that her Client came in to request he be appointed as temporary sole successor trustee because his Sister was not cooperating, that the house was sold, that all of the funds are in a blocked account, that her Client used his own money to repair the house and head off the foreclosure, and that her Client wants to prepare an accounting. In addition, Ms. La stated that there are no other assets other than the house, that the funds will be split, that there is no more trust administration to be done except maybe taxes, and that everything is done except the accounting and disbursement of the funds. The Court authorizes Mr. Siverand to prepare an accounting. Ms. La requested two months to do the accounting. The Petition for Instructions is continued to February 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Ms. La that the accounting is to be filed at least one week prior to the hearing date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Dec-17-2018 Continued To Jan-07-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christine Tour-Sarkissian and Phil Foster for Kelvin Siverand (Petitioner/Trustee).

Mr. Foster provided stamped courtesy copies of the supplemental report stating that the attachments show that the funds are deposited into a blocked account. Mr. Foster also stated that the account is at the Bank of Guam, that it is a savings account in the amount of $400,000.00 and that it is insured up to $500,000.00. The Court pointed out that what was presented does not show that the account is a blocked account. Mr. Foster stated that the bank informed him that it can not split the money up without a Court order. The Court informed Counsel that the receipt for the blocked account needs to be on the Judicial Council Form. Status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account and the petition for instructions are continued to January 7, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Anasse. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Dec-17-2018 Continued To Jan-07-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Dec-03-2018 Continued To Dec-17-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christine Tour-Sarkissian for Kelvin Siverand (Co-Trustee).

Ms. Sarkissian stated that they closed escrow on Friday, that the funds are supposed to be wired to the Bank of Guam in San Francisco and that they anticipate receiving around $300,000.00. The Court explained that the funds will have to be deposited into two different banks to make sure the funds are federally insured. Ms. Sarkissian stated that the balance will be deposited into Bank of America. The Court instructed Ms. Sarkissian to file a supplement indicating what banks are going to be used. Said supplement is to be filed within one week. Status on filing of the receipts for the blocked accounts and the Petition for Instructions are continued to December 17, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Dec-03-2018 Continued To Dec-17-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Sep-24-2018 Continued To Dec-03-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trang La for Kelvin Siverand (Trustee).

Mr. La stated that the real estate agent is showing the house four times a week, that the agent said things are slow and that they got the foreclosure sale postponed to December 19th. Mr. La also stated that once the house is sold, the funds will go in a blocked account, that they petition to Court for reimbursement of the costs his Client paid for and that then will split everything 50/50. The Court informed Counsel that he needs to deposit the funds into multiple banks and that it wants a supplement indicating which banks will be used. Status on filing of the receipt for the blocked accounts and the Petition for Instructions are continued to December 3, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Sep-24-2018 Continued To Dec-03-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Jul-16-2018 Continued To Sep-24-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christine Tour-Sarkissian and Trang La for and with Kelvin Siverand.
Cynthia Taylor (Co-Trustee/Beneficiary) in Propria Persona.
Nicole Taylor (Cynthia's Daughter) also present.

Ms. Sarkissian expressed concern stating that the foreclosure was recorded on May 3rd, that she is expecting to receive notice of Trustee sale on August 3rd, and that her Client has tried to communicate with the reverse mortgage company, but no one is willing to speak with him because he is not the sole Trustee. Ms. Sarkissian also stated that they are trying to get a forbearance on the sale, that the lender will not speak with them because they do not have an order from the Court as sole Trustee, that they are trying to stop the sale and that if there is no trust between the Parties, they can put the proceeds from the sale in a blocked account. Cynthia Taylor stated that she signed papers giving Mr. Siverand permission to sell the property and that she has no objection to selling the property. Nicole Taylor stated that her Sister filed an conservatorship matter, that her Mother signed the papers giving permission to sell in January, that the house was not cleared out on time and that it went into foreclosure. Ms. Sarkissian asked to have her Client appointed as Trustee Ad Litem so that they can get a forbearance and get a Broker for listing of the property. Nicole Taylor asked if an accountant is going to look over the expenses incurred. The Court granted the Petition for Instructions (subject to review of the order). The funds from the sale of the property are to be deposited into a blocked account. Status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account is set for September 24, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

July 18, 2018: The Court signed an order appointing Kelvin Siverand as Sole Temporary Trustee pending the outcome of the purported conservatorship of Cynthia Taylor. The Petition for Instructions is to September 24, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 per Judge Stewart.

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Continued To Jul-16-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Continued To Jun-25-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
